message:
perf: Parallelize Phase 3 test execution for 3x-6x speedup

Problem:
- Tests in Phase 3 ran serially, one at a time
- Each test waited the full 5s timeout even if faster
- @core (288 tests) took 24 minutes: 288 × 5s = 1440s
- CI feedback was extremely slow

Solution:
- Implement parallel worker pool for Phase 3 (same pattern as Phase 1/2)
- Tests now run in parallel using ncpu workers
- Track failed/timeout test names with mutex-protected list
- Print failure summary at end for debugging

Performance Impact:
- Local (10 workers): 6 tests in 8s vs 30s serial (3.75x faster)
- CI ubuntu-latest (4 workers): Expected 288 tests in ~4-5 min vs 24 min
- CI overall: Expected ~10 min total vs 30+ min

Changes:
- Replace serial for loop with RunContext worker pool
- Use atomic counters for thread-safe result tracking
- Track failed test names in ArrayList with Mutex protection
- Print detailed failure list at end: [FAIL/TIMEOUT/COMPILE] test_name
- Keep 5s timeout per test (safety) but now runs parallel

Testing:
- Verified locally with 3 and 6 tests
- Same pass/fail counts as serial execution
- Output interleaved (expected with parallel)
- Final summary accurate

Applies to all test runs (local + all CI platforms).
